_meta.description: MLB park factors for all 30 teams. Based on 3-year rolling Statcast data (2023-2025) and ESPN/FantasyPros research. 100 = league average. >100 = hitter-friendly, <100 = pitcher-friendly. Updated for 2026 venue changes (Rays back to Tropicana, Royals renovated Kauffman, A's still in Sacramento).
_meta.usage: run_factor = park_factors[team_name]['runs'] / 100.0; adjust total or win probability accordingly

Arizona Diamondbacks.notes: Retractable roof, humidor installed. Suppresses HR despite warm air. Boosts hits/doubles.

Baltimore Orioles.notes: Post-2022 dimension changes made it pitcher-friendly. Fences adjusted again for 2025 — trending back toward neutral. RHB HR suppressed heavily (lowest in MLB 2022-2024).

Boston Red Sox.notes: Green Monster inflates doubles/hits. Strong hitter park for runs. LHB HR suppressed, RHB HR boosted.

Chicago Cubs.notes: Trending pitcher-friendly in recent years. Wind direction matters enormously game-to-game but averages out to slight suppression.

Chicago White Sox.notes: Above-average HR park, especially for RHB. Boosts scoring.

Cincinnati Reds.notes: Elite HR park — top 2 in MLB. Small dimensions, Ohio River wind patterns carry fly balls. Major run inflator.

Colorado Rockies.notes: Highest run-scoring venue in MLB by a wide margin. Altitude (5,280 ft) thins air, balls carry farther, breaking balls break less. Humidor partially mitigates but still extreme.

Detroit Tigers.notes: Slightly pitcher-friendly. Deep center field suppresses HR. Spacious outfield.

Houston Astros.notes: Retractable roof. Short left field (315 ft) boosts RHB HR. Crawford Boxes are a factor.

Kansas City Royals.notes: 2026 renovation: outfield walls moved in 8-10 ft and lowered. Previously boosted doubles/triples with vast outfield. HR may increase with new dimensions. Midwest heat helps carry in summer.

Los Angeles Angels.notes: Above-average HR park. Marine layer less impactful than Dodger Stadium due to inland location.

Los Angeles Dodgers.notes: Significant run suppressor despite boosting HR. Marine layer, large foul territory, spacious outfield reduce hits. One of MLB's best pitcher parks for overall scoring.

Miami Marlins.notes: Trending hitter-friendly in recent years despite retractable roof. Was pitcher-friendly early in its existence but has shifted significantly.

Milwaukee Brewers.notes: Above-average HR park. Retractable roof keeps conditions controlled. Slight scoring boost.

Minnesota Twins.notes: Almost perfectly neutral. Outdoor park in Minneapolis.

New York Mets.notes: Pitcher-friendly. Suppresses runs, hits, and HR. One of the more significant run suppressors in MLB.

New York Yankees.notes: Short right field porch (314 ft) inflates LHB HR significantly. Above-average HR park overall.

Athletics.notes: 2025-2026 temporary home. Minor league park with less vertical structure — wind has more impact. Sacramento heat boosts fly ball carry. More hitter-friendly than old Oakland Coliseum.

Philadelphia Phillies.notes: Above-average HR park. Boosts power, especially to right-center. Consistent run inflator.

Pittsburgh Pirates.notes: Boosts runs and hits but suppresses HR. Spacious outfield yields doubles/triples. River wind patterns can carry balls or knock them down depending on direction.

San Diego Padres.notes: Pitcher-friendly. Marine layer and spacious dimensions suppress scoring. Less extreme than pre-2013 but still below average.

San Francisco Giants.notes: One of MLB's strongest HR suppressors. McCovey Cove wind and marine layer kill fly balls. Extreme RHB HR suppression. Pitcher's park overall.

Seattle Mariners.notes: Lowest or second-lowest run-scoring park in MLB. Marine layer, retractable roof (rarely closed), and spacious dimensions all suppress offense.

St. Louis Cardinals.notes: Slightly below neutral. Midwest heat in summer can help carry but overall plays close to average.

Tampa Bay Rays.notes: 2026 return to Tropicana Field after 2025 in Steinbrenner Field. Indoor dome with artificial turf. Historically slightly pitcher-friendly. Catwalk/ring interference is unique.

Texas Rangers.notes: Retractable roof. Above-average HR park. Was a massive hitter park in 2020 opening year, has normalized but still boosts power.

Toronto Blue Jays.notes: Retractable roof. Slight HR boost. Mostly neutral for runs. Artificial turf can speed up grounders.

Washington Nationals.notes: 2026 temporary home (RFK). No MLB park factor history — set to neutral 100 pending data.
